dag.dag_name: Orders fulfillment task-mapping saturates the Celery executor pool

tasks.dependencies: Fetches the Store Sales entity list (stores/zones/segments/vendors) to fan out over
One mapped instance per entity, bound to a dedicated Airflow pool
Runs in parallel with process_unit, watches shared pool capacity
Fan-in of all mapped instances plus the monitor
Evaluates whether queue congestion impacted this or sibling DAGs

incident.incident_name: Orders fulfillment task-mapping saturates the Celery executor pool
incident.description: Queue Congestion incident on the Store Sales pipeline (RTL_STS_QUEUE): orders fulfillment task-mapping saturates the Celery executor pool.
incident.symptoms: A dynamically mapped fulfillment-routing task (one mapped instance per pending order, ~8,000 during a sale) queued for 2h before the last instances began executing.
incident.root_cause: The DAG's mapped task count exceeded the Celery worker pool's total slot capacity (200) by 40x with no pool assignment, so it competed directly with every other production DAG sharing the default pool.
incident.business_impact: Every other DAG sharing the default Celery pool experienced queuing delays, turning a single order-spike event into a platform-wide slowdown affecting 15+ unrelated DAGs.
incident.resolution: Assign this DAG's mapped tasks to a dedicated Airflow pool sized appropriately, and add max_active_tis_per_dag to bound how many mapped instances run concurrently.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: The DAG's mapped task count exceeded the Celery worker pool's total slot capacity (200) by 40x with no pool assignment, so it competed directly with every other production DAG sharing the default pool.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow UI Pools view shows 8,000 queued task instances against 200 total slots
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog airflow.pool.open_slots for the default pool hit 0 and stayed there for 2h
expected_rca.resolution: Assign this DAG's mapped tasks to a dedicated Airflow pool sized appropriately, and add max_active_tis_per_dag to bound how many mapped instances run concurrently.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Require every DAG in the metadata model to declare a target Airflow pool and a max-concurrency value at registration; default (shared) pool usage should require an explicit exception.
